summary: 2026-06-08 09:29:13 PDT Review found an IMPORTANT edge-case bug in the drift classifier. The diff is otherwise scoped to `install.sh` plus the story file, and `bash -n install.sh` / `git diff --check main...HEAD` passed.

findings.title: Unusable manifest commits are misclassified as hand edits
findings.claim: `classify_drift` only treats an empty or literal `unknown` commit as unclassifiable. If the manifest records a SHA that is not present in this checkout, or otherwise cannot be archived, the `git archive ... | tar ...` condition fails and the artifact is reported as `HAND-EDITED`. That is not grounded in the deployed content: the script never successfully compared against the recorded commit, so `--check` and the pre-overwrite summary can falsely warn that local edits will be destroyed. This misses the requested unknown-commit edge case and weakens AC5's STALE/HAND-EDITED classification.
findings.suggestion: Before archiving, verify the recorded commit is usable, e.g. `git -C "$SRC" cat-file -e "$commit^{tree}"`; if verification or archive extraction fails, return `UNCLASSIFIED` with a clear unavailable-recorded-commit message instead of falling through to `HAND-EDITED`.
